workflows.name: Initial cull
workflows.primary_command: mt organize <photo-dir> && mt ratings-apply <photo-dir> --manifest ratings.tsv && mt lr-plan <photo-dir> --ratings ">=3" --output lr_plan.tsv && mt lr-apply <photo-dir> --ratings ">=3" --plan lr_plan_reviewed.tsv && mt subject-plan <photo-dir> --ratings ">=3" --output subject_plan.tsv --preview-dir /tmp/mt-subject-review && mt subject-apply <photo-dir> --plan subject_plan_reviewed.tsv --dry-run && mt subject-apply <photo-dir> --plan subject_plan_reviewed.tsv && mt verify-cull <photo-dir>
workflows.default_behavior: Organize a new shoot, use reviewed manifests for portrait/panorama grouping and complete 0-5 RAW ratings with a strict 4- and 5-star bar and a broad 3-star review pool. Build HIF-only contact sheets, optionally apply a reviewed RAW-histogram Lightroom plan, and review every individual HIF/HEIF/HEIC preview for a per-image Select Subject plan on all 3-5-star portraits. Strict verification checks XMP ratings and workflow markers.
preflight: Confirm the source photo directory exists.
Run mt status <photo-dir> --json.
Run mt doctor <photo-dir>.
Run mt organize <photo-dir> --dry-run --verbose and review every planned move; destination collisions are hard errors.
Create a complete path/rating TSV, then run mt ratings-apply <photo-dir> --manifest ratings.tsv --dry-run before writing XMP.
Review lr_plan.tsv by path, remove rows incompatible with the selected scene profile, save lr_plan_reviewed.tsv, then pass it to mt lr-apply <photo-dir> --plan lr_plan_reviewed.tsv.
Run mt subject-plan <photo-dir> --ratings ">=3" and open every generated portrait preview; fill every row in subject_plan_reviewed.tsv independently.
Run mt subject-apply <photo-dir> --plan subject_plan_reviewed.tsv --dry-run before writing the reviewed Select Subject corrections.
must_not: Do not delete rejected RAW or HIF files without a separate explicit request.
Do not copy HIF files to an archive destination.
Do not import Lightroom exports into Apple Photos.
Do not use Lightroom Export JPG files as culling previews.
Do not hand-move ordinary scenes into ad-hoc folders; only portrait and panorama grouping have reviewed manifest movers.
Do not claim automatic ISO, lens, white-balance, crop, Upright, sharpening, or noise-reduction tuning.
Do not claim Media Toolkit computes Select Subject pixels; Lightroom computes the AI mask after metadata is read.
agent_notes: Use HIF/HEIF/HEIC as visual previews and RAW statistics as exposure evidence.
Group portraits by person and panorama frames by sequence using reviewed manifests.
Keep the 4- and 5-star bar strict, broaden the 3-star pool when near-duplicates have meaningful differences, and run the full portrait treatment for all 3-5-star portraits.
Use mt verify-cull <photo-dir>; --legacy-structure-only is for old folders, not new culls.

workflows.name: Finalize refined photos
workflows.default_behavior: Use Lightroom exports as the final-pick list, copy matching original HIF/HEIF/HEIC files to an explicit external destination, and import final Export images into Apple Photos unless --hif-only is used. Optional cleanup starts with mt hif-prune <photo-dir> --mode plan; permanent deletion is a separate user-confirmed action using --mode aggressive --apply-plan <reviewed.json> --confirm-delete so the reviewed plan is not rebuilt.
preflight: Confirm source photo directory exists.
Run mt status <photo-dir> --workflow finalize --copy-to <destination-dir> --json.
Run mt doctor <photo-dir> --workflow finalize --copy-to <destination-dir>.
Run mt preflight-run finalize <photo-dir> --copy-to <destination-dir> --scene <scene> for a read-only recursive GO/NO-GO check.
Require --copy-to and verify it is outside the user-supplied source root.
Review missing matches and same-name content conflicts; either makes HIF archiving fail.
After finalization, run mt hif-prune <photo-dir> --mode plan only when source cleanup was requested; review the manifest before any deletion.
must_not: Do not infer a destination from a single provided path.
Do not use the source directory, a child directory, featured/, ~/Downloads/featured, or a remembered path as the destination.
Do not copy Lightroom Export or Pixcake JPG files to the HIF destination.
Do not copy panorama source-frame HIF files from panorama/<n>/hif/.
Do not overwrite a same-named destination file with different content.
Do not treat finalization as permission to delete source files.
agent_notes: Recursive scene roots are the default.
Export/Pixcake has Photos-import priority over a same-stem direct Export file.
Photos exposes submission success, not reliable duplicate-skip or final imported counts.

workflows.name: Learn refined color style
workflows.default_behavior: Read final Export/XMP pairs recursively, prefer Pixcake evidence, summarize frequencies and numeric min/median/max, and compare observed values with an optional existing scene baseline. The command is read-only and writes no report into the photo directory.
preflight: Confirm the photo directory contains final Lightroom Export files and matching XMP sidecars.
Run mt styles before selecting an existing baseline profile.
Use mt learn-style <photo-dir> --scene <scene> --baseline <profile> --json when a baseline comparison is meaningful.
must_not: Do not organize media or write ratings/XMP.
Do not finalize, copy, import, or delete media.
Do not fall back silently to every RAW/XMP when final exports are absent.
Do not create per-photo-directory learning reports.
Do not generalize one scene into a universal style.
agent_notes: Rating-only XMP is ignored as style evidence.
Update repository profiles or prompts only when the user requests it and the lesson is stable.

workflows.name: Apple Photos location fill
workflows.default_behavior: Scan Apple Photos read-only, compare previous and next located timeline neighbors, write an auditable HTML/JSON plan, and apply only the exact reviewed JSON in a separate explicitly confirmed stage.
preflight: Run mt fill-locations --force-refresh, or use a bounded --start/--end/--scan-start query.
Review the latest HTML row, warnings, source read errors, both neighbor deltas, and the exact JSON plan.
Use mt fill-locations --apply-plan <reviewed-plan.json> only after explicit approval.
must_not: Do not combine planning and writing with --apply; that legacy path is disabled.
Do not apply an interrupted, truncated, malformed, or partially reviewed plan.
Do not rescan Photos or rebuild source choices during --apply-plan.
Do not claim visual similarity is implemented; source selection is timestamp-based.
agent_notes: Coordinate rows are schema-, range-, and finiteness-validated before AppleScript runs.
Reuse a reviewed cache unless the user requests a refresh.
Large Photos queries may take several minutes.
